description: Gold set for the qa topic matcher (_same_qa_topic / _qa_topic_groups) ONLY — not a retrieval benchmark. Motivated by the 2026-07-13 httpx EN holdout: update newer_first collapsed 6/6→1/6 because topic grouping was calibrated on Korean token statistics. relation=same → matcher must group; relation=adjacent → matcher must NOT group (false-group here causes the worst failure: a fresh adjacent answer steals the guaranteed slot from an old exact answer); relation=bridge → three items where a≈b and b≈c look plausible but a and c must NEVER land in one group (anti-chaining). Entries with known_limitation=true are pure cross-language pairs a lexical matcher cannot bridge — reported, excluded from the pass gate. Mixed-language same-topic pairs group via shared ASCII identifiers (realistic: Korean dev Q&A embeds English identifiers).

a.query: how many retries does the transport do
a.answer: The transport is created with retries=0; every retry lives in the app layer.

b.answer: We retried connect errors in the transport itself now: HTTPTransport(retries=3). App-layer retry removed.

a.query: what is the default request timeout
a.answer: Default timeout is 5 seconds for connect, read, write and pool.

b.query: default timeouts raised for slow upstream
b.answer: Timeout default went from 5 to 30 seconds via httpx.Timeout(30.0).

a.query: what connection limits do we run with
a.answer: Pooling uses max_connections=100 and max_keepalive_connections=20.

b.query: connections pool tuned down after fd incident
b.answer: We now pass httpx.Limits(max_connections=50, max_keepalive_connections=10) explicitly.

a.query: how does authentication work for the admin api
a.answer: Admin authentication uses signed Authorization headers minted by the gateway.

b.answer: We authenticate admin calls with short-lived signed headers now; long-lived keys are revoked.

a.answer: Yes, the client follows redirect chains up to 20 hops by default.

b.answer: The client no longer follows redirects; a redirected response now raises so callers handle Location explicitly.

a.answer: Pooling keeps 20 keepalive connections with a 5 second expiry.

b.answer: Keepalive expiry in the pool dropped from 5s to 1s to cycle stale sockets faster.

a.answer: raise_for_status raises HTTPStatusError on any 4xx or 5xx response.

b.answer: We changed raise_for_status usage: 4xx is tolerated, HTTPStatusError raised only for 5xx.

a.query: why is tls verification disabled in staging
a.answer: Staging uses self-signed certs so the client is built with verify=False.

b.answer: Staging now passes verify='/etc/ssl/internal-ca.pem'; the verify=False client is gone.

a.query: how is the outbound proxy configured
a.answer: trust_env=True and the deployment sets HTTP_PROXY / HTTPS_PROXY.

b.answer: Client now gets proxy='http://egress.internal:3128' with trust_env=False; env vars ignored.

a.query: is http2 enabled on our client
a.answer: No h2 extra installed; the client speaks HTTP/1.1 only.

b.answer: httpx[http2] installed and the factory passes http2=True everywhere.

a.query: where do we set the api client base url
a.answer: base_url comes from settings.API_BASE_URL in the client factory.

b.query: base url moved to per-tenant config
b.answer: The factory now resolves base_url per tenant instead of the global settings.API_BASE_URL.

a.query: how do we stream large file downloads
a.answer: client.stream with iter_bytes(chunk_size=65536) written straight to disk.

b.answer: Streaming downloads now use iter_bytes(chunk_size=262144); 64k chunks were too chatty.

a.query: how do we mock httpx in unit tests
a.answer: Tests pass a handler to httpx.MockTransport returning canned Response objects.

b.answer: The MockTransport handler moved into a shared pytest fixture; individual tests stopped building their own.

a.query: what is the upload size limit
a.answer: Uploads are capped at 50MB by the ingress buffer.

b.answer: Upload cap went from 50MB to 500MB after disk-backed spooling landed.

a.query: which ca bundle does production use
a.answer: Production mounts /etc/ssl/internal-ca.pem as the CA bundle for outbound calls.

b.answer: The internal-ca.pem bundle was reissued; old chain expires next month, path unchanged.

a.query: why did the unit test hit its timeout
a.answer: The integration-marked unit test exceeded the 30s pytest timeout on CI runners.

b.answer: The database fixture now tears down after the cache fixture to avoid dangling handles.

a.answer: Connect failures retry 3 times with exponential backoff in the transport.

b.answer: Every request is logged as structured JSON with method, path and duration.

a.answer: SSL configuration lives in the client factory: verify path and minimum TLS version.

b.answer: SSL handshake errors are grouped in Sentry under a dedicated fingerprint for alerting.

a.query: what are the connection pool limits
a.answer: The pool caps at 50 connections with 10 keepalive.

b.query: why do we strip the connection header
b.answer: The Connection header is hop-by-hop, so the proxy strips it before forwarding.

a.query: what timeout does the client use
a.answer: The shared client uses a 30 second total timeout.

b.answer: Clients authenticate with mTLS certificates issued by the internal CA.

a.query: how do we stream large downloads
a.answer: Downloads stream via iter_bytes and never buffer the whole body.

b.answer: Uploads are capped at 500MB with disk-backed spooling at the ingress.

a.query: how is the egress proxy configured
a.answer: Outbound traffic goes through egress.internal:3128 set in code with trust_env=False.

b.answer: Reference documentation moved to docs.internal/api-v2; old links redirect for 90 days.

a.answer: Redirects are not followed; callers read Location and decide.

b.query: url routing table for the admin panel
b.answer: Admin panel routes are declared in routes.py with one entry per screen.

a.answer: Access tokens expire after 15 minutes; refresh happens transparently.

b.query: what is the session cookie called
b.answer: The session cookie is __Host-session with Secure and HttpOnly set.

a.query: what are the pool limits in production
a.answer: Production pools cap at max_connections=50.

b.query: how many worker processes do we run
b.answer: The app runs 8 gunicorn workers per node, capped by memory.

a.answer: Outbound requests time out after 30 seconds total.

b.answer: The CI pipeline job timeout went from 10 to 20 minutes for the e2e suite.

a.answer: Yes, http2=True with the h2 extra installed.

b.answer: Upstream 502/503/504 map to a single retryable error class in our handler.

a.query: how do we mock the transport in tests
a.answer: httpx.MockTransport with a canned handler replaces the network.

b.answer: Seed scripts insert mock rows into the dev database via factory_boy.

a.query: where is the base url set
a.answer: base_url is injected by the client factory from tenant config.

b.query: which base image does the dockerfile use
b.answer: The Dockerfile builds from python:3.12-slim as the base image.

a.query: what is the retry backoff policy
a.answer: Retries back off exponentially starting at 250ms with jitter.

b.answer: Nightly backups are retained for 30 days in cold storage.

items.query: what is the default timeout value
items.answer: The default timeout is 30 seconds.

items.query: how do timeout and retry interact
items.answer: Each retry attempt gets its own timeout budget; retries never extend the deadline.

items.query: what is the retry backoff curve
items.answer: Retry backoff is exponential from 250ms with full jitter.
The pool caps at max_connections=50.

items.query: how do keepalive connections get reused
items.answer: Idle keepalive connections in the pool are reused for up to 10 requests.

items.query: what keepalive probes does the os send
items.answer: TCP keepalive probes fire after 60s idle at the kernel level, unrelated to the HTTP pool.
Login tokens expire after 15 minutes.

items.query: how does token refresh rotation work
items.answer: Refresh rotates the token pair on every use; reuse of an old refresh token revokes the session.

items.query: how often does the dashboard cache refresh
items.answer: The dashboard cache refreshes every 5 minutes via a background job.

items.query: is ssl verification on in staging
items.answer: Yes, staging verifies against the internal CA bundle.

items.query: where is the ssl cert bundle
items.answer: The cert bundle is mounted at /etc/ssl/internal-ca.pem.
Cert expiry alerts page the on-call channel 30 days before expiry.
Downloads stream in 256KB chunks to disk.
Uploads use chunked transfer encoding when the body size is unknown.

items.query: what encoding do csv exports use
items.answer: CSV exports are UTF-8 with BOM for Excel compatibility.

a.answer: 정산 배치는 매일 새벽 2시(KST), cron 0 2 * * * 로 실행됩니다.

items.answer: 기본 timeout은 httpx.Timeout(30.0)입니다.
Each retry gets its own timeout budget.
retry는 250ms부터 지수 백오프에 지터를 더합니다.
pool은 max_connections=50이 상한입니다.

rotation 이벤트 로그는 감사 목적으로 1년 보관합니다.
